title: elytraria planifolia subsp. planifolia
intro: Elytraria planifolia subsp. planifolia, also known as blue curls, is a species of flowering plant in the family Acanthaceae. It is native to the southeastern United States, particularly in the states of Alabama, Florida, and Louisiana. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, and it is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever and promoting wound healing. The herbal preparations of blue curls include teas and infusions, which can be used to treat various health issues, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
